---
title: "Results Draft 1"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{booktabs}
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{threeparttable}
          \usepackage{ragged2e}
---

```{r, echo = F, include = F, warning = F, message = F}
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(kSamples)
library(tibble)
library(kableExtra)

decision_space_raw <- read_csv(here("data/decision_space/decision_space_raw.csv"))
decision_space <- read_csv(here("data/decision_space/decision_space.csv"))
sampled_universes <- readRDS(here("data/sampled_universes/sampled_universes_100.RDS"))
results <- readRDS(here("data/results/all_models_final_results_Univ100_6400_2026-04-20.RDS"))

# Make sure all NAs are seen as NAs
results <- results %>%
  mutate(RR = ifelse(model_type == "GEE" & (RR == Inf | RR == 0), NA, RR),
         conf.low = ifelse(model_type == "GEE" & (conf.low == Inf | conf.low == 0), NA, conf.low),
         conf.high = ifelse(model_type == "GEE" & (conf.high == Inf | conf.high == 0), NA, conf.high))

# How are NAs distributed per statistical model?
NAperModel <- results %>%
  group_by(model_type) %>%
  summarise(na_count = sum(is.na(RR)))

minmaxRR <- results |>
  filter(!is.na(RR)) |>
  summarise(minRR = round(min(RR),2),
            maxRR = round(max(RR),2))
```

# Results
### Distribution of Risk Ratios Across Decisions
Out of the `r nrow(sampled_universes)` sampled universes, `r sum(is.na(results$RR))` statistical models did not converge, with `r NAperModel$na_count[NAperModel$model_type == "log-binom"]` log-binomial models, `r NAperModel$na_count[NAperModel$model_type == "GEE"]` generalised estimating equations (GEE) and `r NAperModel$na_count[NAperModel$model_type == "log_reg"]` logistic regression analyses resulting in non-convergence. The other three models converged in all universes. Across the other decisions, NAs in the results were distributed equally. In total, `r nrow(sampled_universes) - sum(is.na(results$RR))` universes successfully resulted in risk ratios.

Across all `r nrow(sampled_universes) - sum(is.na(results$RR))` specifications, RRs ranged from `r minmaxRR$minRR` to `r minmaxRR$maxRR`, indicating that the effect of marriage on the risk of a cardiovascular event ranged from negative to positive within this sample of the full multiverse. As shown in @fig-spec-curve, the effect estimates and their significance varied across the `r nrow(sampled_universes) - sum(is.na(results$RR))` universes. 



Table @tbl-RRsensitivity summarises the sensitivity of results to each analytical decision, by showing the range of the mean RR across decision options and the percentage of significant results across options. Model choice and adjustment sets had the largest impact on effect estimates (RR range: 0.83–1.12 and 0.83–1.07, respectively), while missing data handling and exclusion criteria had minimal impact (RR range: 0.94–0.95 for both).

All mean RRs per decision option can be found in @tbl-RRfulltable (@sec-appendix). The largest RR variation was found in the statistical model decision, where the mean RR ranged from 0.83 (Cox PH) to 1.12 (GEE) and in the adjustment set, where the mean RR ranged from 0.83 (for $ADJ \in \text{\{Smoke + Sex\} or \{Country + Sex\} or \{Sex\}}$) to 1.07 (for $ADJ \in \text{\{Age + Smoke + Physical activity\}}$). 

```{r}
#| label: tbl-ADtest
#| tbl-cap: "K-sample Anderson-Darling tests for distributional differences across analytical decision options."
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| echo: false
#| 
set.seed(260722)
DT_AD <- ad.test(RR ~ data_type, data = results)
MD_AD <- ad.test(RR ~ missing_data, data = results)
EC_AD <- ad.test(RR ~ exclusion, data = results)
OUT_AD <- ad.test(RR ~ outcome_label, data = results)
ADJ_AD <- ad.test(RR ~ adjustment_label, data = results)
SM_AD <- ad.test(RR ~ model_type, data = results)

ADtable <- tibble(
  Decision = c("Data Type", "Missing Data", "Exclusion Criteria", 
               "Outcome", "Statistical Model", "Adjustment Set"), 
  `N options` = c(DT_AD$k, MD_AD$k, EC_AD$k, OUT_AD$k, SM_AD$k, ADJ_AD$k),
   AD = c(DT_AD$ad[1,1], MD_AD$ad[1,1], EC_AD$ad[1,1], 
                     OUT_AD$ad[1,1], SM_AD$ad[1,1], ADJ_AD$ad[1,1]),
  `T.AD` = c(DT_AD$ad[1,2], MD_AD$ad[1,2], EC_AD$ad[1,2], 
             OUT_AD$ad[1,2], SM_AD$ad[1,2], ADJ_AD$ad[1,2]),
  p_value = c(DT_AD$ad[1,3], MD_AD$ad[1,3], EC_AD$ad[1,3], 
                OUT_AD$ad[1,3], SM_AD$ad[1,3], ADJ_AD$ad[1,3])
)

ADtable <- ADtable %>%
  mutate(
    p_value = case_when(
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "< .001",
      TRUE ~ paste0(round(p_value, 3))
    )
  )

ADtable %>%
  kbl(
    booktabs = TRUE,
    linesep = "",
    digits = 2,
    col.names = c("Decision", "k", "AD", "T.AD", "p-value"),
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    position = "left",
    font_size = 11,
    latex_options = "hold_position"
  ) %>%
  footnote(
    general = "k = number of options; T.AD = standardised Anderson-Darling statistic.",
    general_title = "Note. ",
    threeparttable = TRUE,
    footnote_as_chunk = TRUE
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size = 8)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-spec-curve
#| fig-cap: "Specification curve multiverse results"
#| echo: false
#| fig-align: "center"

knitr::include_graphics("specification_curve.pdf")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-dens-plot
#| fig-cap: "Densities of RR across decisions and options"
#| echo: false
#| out-width: "100%"
#| fig-align: "center"
#| fig-pos: "H"

knitr::include_graphics("density_plot.png")
```

# Appendix {#sec-appendix .appendix}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-RRfulltable
#| tbl-cap: "RRs, their range and percentage of significant RRs across decisions and their options."
#| warning: false
#| include: true
#| echo: false

decisions <- c("model_type", "data_type", "missing_data", "exclusion", "outcome_label", "adjustment_label")

# Create label mappings
decision_labels <- c(
  "data_type" = "Data Type",
  "missing_data" = "Missing Data",
  "exclusion" = "Exclusion Criteria",
  "outcome_label" = "Outcome",
  "model_type" = "Statistical Model",
  "adjustment_label" = "Adjustment Set"
)

option_labels <- c(
  # Data type
  "cross_all" = "Cross-sectional (all waves)",
  "cross_w1" = "Cross-sectional (wave 1)",
  "long_nostrict" = "Longitudinal (no strict baseline)",
  "long_w1" = "Longitudinal (wave 1)",
  
  # Missing data
  "cc" = "Complete cases",
  "ni" = "No information",
  
  # Exclusion
  "born_after1954" = "Born after 1954",
  "country" = "Country",
  "divorced_widowed" = "Divorced/widowed",
  "married_before18" = "Married before 18",
  "none" = "None",
  "younger_than_55_w1" = "Younger than 55",
  
  # Model type
  "cox_haz" = "Cox PH",
  "log_reg" = "Logistic regression",
  "log-binom" = "Log-binomial",
  "mix_log_reg" = "Mixed effects logistic regression",
  "time_mix" = "Discrete-time mixed effects",
  "GEE" = "GEE"
)

# Create table with nice labels
full_table <- map_dfr(decisions, function(d) {
  results %>%
    filter(!is.na(RR)) %>%
    group_by(Option = .data[[d]]) %>%
    summarise(
      `N` = n(),
      `Mean RR` = round(mean(RR), 2),
      `RR Range` = paste0("[", round(min(RR), 2), ", ", round(max(RR), 2), "]"),
      `% Sig` = paste0(round(mean(p.value < 0.05) * 100, 0), "%")
    ) %>%
    arrange(`Mean RR`, ,
      .groups = "drop")|>
    mutate(
      Decision = decision_labels[d],
      Option = ifelse(Option %in% names(option_labels), option_labels[Option], Option)
    ) %>%
    select(Decision, Option, N, `Mean RR`, `RR Range`, `% Sig`)
})

# Clean adjustment labels in the data first
full_table <- full_table %>%
  mutate(
    Option = Option %>%
      str_replace_all("dn010_", "Edu") %>%
      str_replace_all("dn042_", "Sex") %>%
      str_replace_all("br001_", "Smoke") %>%
      str_replace_all("phactiv", "Phys") %>%
      str_replace_all("country", "Country") %>%
      str_replace_all("age_int", "Age") %>%
      str_replace_all("none", "None") %>%
      str_replace_all("since_last_int", "Since") |>
      str_replace_all("age_at_event", "Age event") %>%
      str_replace_all("death_by_event", "Death") %>%
      str_replace_all("event_ever", "Event ever") %>%
      str_replace_all("\\+", " + ")
  )

full_table %>%
  kbl(
    booktabs = TRUE,
    longtable = TRUE,
    linesep = "",
    align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "c", "r"),
    col.names = c("Decision", "Option", "Universes", "Mean RR", "RR Range", "% Sig")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("repeat_header", "hold_position"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 6.5  
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "2.5cm") %>%  # Decision column width
  column_spec(2, width = "6cm") %>%    # Option column - wider for long text
  column_spec(3, width = "1.2cm") %>%  # N
  column_spec(4, width = "1.5cm") %>%  # Mean RR
  column_spec(5, width = "2cm") %>%    # RR Range
  column_spec(6, width = "1.2cm") %>%  # % Sig
  collapse_rows(
    columns = 1,
    valign = "top",
    latex_hline = "major"
  )
```
